description: A dot strip plot: one Circle mark per detail-grain member placed along an aggregated-measure axis, grouped by a category on rows, with a per-cell average reference line — a space-efficient way to rank/compare members within each row. Lane W11-F2: the source month filter was PARAMETERIZED (collapsed to level-members) so the date stays a data-driven Filters pill with no source literals.

slots.notes: Month of the bound date on the rows axis — one strip per month. Used at a single derivation (mn), so no qualified key is required.
Aggregated measure on the cols axis — the value each dot is placed at; also the per-cell average reference line.
Level-of-detail dimension — one Circle mark per member within each row's strip.

hazards.detail: The source workbook pinned Month of Order Date to a 1..6 (Jan–Jun) member list — a source-calendar window that would render empty/partial on a differently-shaped date. Lane F2's compile-time filter policy PARAMETERIZED it: the six-member union was collapsed to the neutral all-members form (<groupfilter function='level-members' level='[mn:Order Date:ok]'/>), so Month stays a data-driven Filters-shelf pill (its <slices> entry is preserved) with the source literals removed. This is the same neutral form the apply-path 3c neutralizer writes, so compile + apply agree (idempotent).
